title: Launch page: hero copy + one-liner
description: Final hero copy for the launch page. Blocked on the product one-liner. Design ready to lay out once words land.

title: Release notes + known-limitations doc for launch
description: Compile launch release notes including any known limitations (e.g., Connections edge cases).

title: Slack Connection drops OAuth token mid-session
description: Meeting→task push to Slack fails intermittently (~1 in 5) when the OAuth token needs a refresh mid-session. Connections is a headline launch feature; cannot ship silently broken.

title: Daily Summaries: prompt quality pass before launch
description: Improve Daily Summaries output quality ahead of launch — strong proof point for the brief narrative.

text: Raising to P0 — this is on the launch page. Scope question before we commit to blocking launch: is proactive re-auth enough, or do we also need a graceful 'reconnect' state for the case where Slack revokes server-side? I'd want both before I call this launch-safe.
Aligned with Samantha on the wedge: lead with the proactive brief, not 'capture.' Drafting 3 one-liner options tonight, decision tomorrow AM so @sam isn't blocked.
Both in scope. Proactive re-auth ~5min before expiry, plus a real 'reconnect Slack' state for server-side revoke. Patch tonight.
PR #318 up. 0 failures / 200 runs. Server-side revoke now surfaces 'reconnect Slack' instead of silent failure.
Approved to ship. Adding a release-notes line for the Slack-revoke edge case. Once #318 merges, move to Done.
Shipped. +18% on the summary-quality eval set. Closing.
Decision: 'Highlight briefs you, then moves your work forward.' Samantha + I aligned, it's the one line a competitor couldn't also claim. @sam unblocked to lay out.
